header.subtitle: An embeddable NA meeting finder widget for any website.

overview.title: Overview
overview.p1: Crumb Widget is a self-contained JavaScript widget that queries a BMLT server and renders a fully featured meeting finder — search, filters, list view, and an interactive map.
overview.p2: It ships as a single JavaScript file with all CSS injected at runtime. No stylesheets, no build steps, no framework required on the host page.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.intro: To get started, add a container element to your page, point it at your BMLT server, and load the widget script. Here is a complete example:
quickStart.autoInit: The widget initializes automatically when the script loads, so no additional JavaScript is needed.
quickStart.viewport: Important: Be sure your page includes <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /> in the <head>. This is important for proper rendering on mobile devices and small screens.

attributes.title: Data Attributes
attributes.intro: All configuration is set as data-* attributes on the #crumb-widget div.
attributes.server:
Full URL to your BMLT server.
Example: https://example.org/main_server
attributes.serviceBody:
Filter meetings to one or more service bodies. Accepts a single numeric ID or a comma-separated list. Omit to show all meetings on the server.
Single: "42"
Multiple: "42,57,103"
Note: recursive lookup is always enabled — child service bodies are automatically included and cannot be disabled.
attributes.view:
Default view when the widget loads.
Values: list (default), map, or both
both shows a fixed-height map above the full meeting list with no list/map toggle.
Can be overridden at runtime by the ?view= query parameter (see URL Query Parameters).
attributes.path:
Enables History API routing (clean URLs without #) and sets the base path. Without this attribute, hash-based routing is used (default).
Example: data-path="/meetings" produces URLs like /meetings/monday-night-meeting-42
Use data-path="" for path routing with no prefix (e.g. in iframe embeds).
See Routing for details.

globalConfig.title: Global Config Object
globalConfig.intro: For options not available as data attributes, define CrumbWidgetConfig as a global variable before loading the script.

row.view: Override the default view. Takes precedence over data-view. 'both' shows a fixed-height map above the full meeting list; the list/map toggle is hidden.
row.language: Override the UI language (e.g. 'es', 'fr'). Defaults to navigator.language, falling back to English. Supported: en, es, fr, de, pt, it, sv, da, el, fa, pl, ru, ja.
row.columns: Columns to show in list view. Default: ['time', 'name', 'location', 'address']. Omit any column name to hide it. Available values: time, name, location (venue/building name), address (street address with in-person/online badges), service_body. service_body is hidden by default — add it explicitly to show the service body name.
row.geolocation: Show a Near Me button and auto-geolocate on page load to find meetings near the user. Requires HTTPS. Default: false. If geolocation fails or is denied, an error is shown — no fallback load occurs. Note: serviceBodyIds filters have no effect on geolocation searches; results are determined entirely by proximity.
row.geolocationRadius: Maximum search radius when using geolocation. Acts as the cap for the Near Me distance dropdown — options above this value are hidden. Default: 75.
row.distanceOptions: Distance values (in distanceUnit) shown in the Near Me dropdown. Options above geolocationRadius are automatically hidden; if geolocationRadius is not in the list it is appended as the last option. Default: [5, 10, 15, 25, 50, 100].
row.distanceUnit: Unit label shown next to distances in the Near Me dropdown. Default: 'mi'.
row.nowOffset: Minutes of grace period before a meeting is considered past. Meetings that started within the last nowOffset minutes still appear at the top of the list; older meetings on today's weekday roll over to the end of the list (as if they occur next week). Default: 10. Set to 0 to roll over immediately once a meeting's start time has passed.
row.hideHeader: Hide the "Meeting Finder" title bar and meeting count at the top of the widget. Default: false.
row.darkMode: Built-in dark color scheme. 'auto' follows the visitor's OS preference (prefers-color-scheme); true forces dark mode regardless of OS setting; false (default) disables it. Works alongside dark mode tiles.
row.mapTiles: Custom map tile provider. Replaces the default OpenStreetMap tiles. See Tile Provider below.
row.mapTilesDark: Alternate tile provider used when prefers-color-scheme: dark. Swaps automatically on OS theme change. See Dark Mode Tiles below.
row.mapMarkers: Custom map marker for meeting locations. Replaces the default NA marker. See Marker Config below.

language.title: Language
language.intro: The widget automatically detects the visitor's language from navigator.language and falls back to English. Override it with the language config property:
language.codes: Language codes are matched on the base tag, so 'fr-CA' uses fr. Supported languages:

geolocation.title: Geolocation
geolocation.p1: Enable the Near Me button with geolocation: true. When enabled, the widget also attempts to geolocate the user automatically on page load and show meetings nearby. Requires a secure context (HTTPS).
geolocation.p2: If geolocation is denied or unavailable, an error message is displayed — there is no silent fallback to loading all meetings. This is intentional: on large servers without service body scoping, loading all meetings could return tens of thousands of results.
geolocation.p3: Service body filters have no effect on geolocation searches. When using Near Me, results are determined entirely by the user's coordinates and geolocationRadius. If no serviceBodyIds are configured, the Near Me button cannot be toggled off — geolocation is the only data source.
geolocation.p4: The Near Me button opens a distance dropdown so users can choose how far to search. Selecting a radius applies a client-side distance filter on the already-loaded meetings — no new network request is made when changing radius. The available distances come from distanceOptions, capped at geolocationRadius; options with no meetings in range are hidden automatically. Use distanceUnit to display kilometres instead of miles.
geolocation.p5: While in map view with Near Me active, panning or zooming the map reveals a Search this area button at the top of the map. Clicking it reloads meetings centered on the current map viewport, using the same selected radius. The map stays at the user's position — it does not jump to fit the new results.

queryParams.title: URL Query Parameters
queryParams.intro: The ?view= query parameter sets the initial view and overrides data-view and view:

row.both: Show map above the full meeting list; list/map toggle is hidden
row.auto: Geolocate on load — success shows map with nearby results; failure falls back to list with all meetings

queryParams.disable: ?view=list and ?view=map disable auto-geolocation even if geolocation: true is set.

cssVars.title: CSS Variables
cssVars.intro: The widget exposes CSS custom properties so you can theme it without touching JavaScript. Set any of these on the #crumb-widget element — only specify the ones you want to override.

row.accentLight: Row hover, active filter panel background
row.surface: Input, button, and dropdown panel backgrounds
row.hover: Neutral hover state (buttons and rows not using accent)
row.divider: Internal row and list divider lines
row.textSecondary: Secondary / muted text (table headers, time, location)
row.textMuted: Very muted text (footer count, empty-state messages)

cssDarkMode.title: Dark Mode
cssDarkMode.intro: The widget ships with a built-in dark palette. Enable it with the darkMode config option — no extra CSS required. Works alongside the dark mode tile option.
cssDarkMode.custom: To use a custom dark palette instead, override the CSS variables directly:

cssClasses.title: CSS Helper Classes
cssClasses.intro: These classes are applied internally and can also be targeted in your own CSS for further customization.

row.btnPrimary: Filled accent buttons — active filter chips, view toggle, Join Meeting

tileConfig.title: Tile Provider
tileConfig.intro: By default the map uses OpenStreetMap tiles. Switch to any Leaflet-compatible tile provider by setting map.tiles.

row.url: Tile URL template. Use {z}, {x}, {y} placeholders (and {r} for retina where supported).
row.attribution: Attribution HTML displayed in the map's attribution control. Required by most tile providers' terms of service.

tileDarkConfig.title: Dark Mode Tiles
tileDarkConfig.intro: Set map.tiles_dark to use a different tile layer for visitors with prefers-color-scheme: dark. The layer swaps automatically when the OS theme changes — no page reload needed. If omitted, the same tiles are used in all color schemes.
tileDarkConfig.googleNote: Google Maps tiles: Google's tile API requires a session token obtained server-side (or via a short-lived client-side fetch). Because the token must be fetched before CrumbWidgetConfig is set, you need to load https://cdn.aws.bmlt.app/crumb-widget.js dynamically after the token is ready. The example below uses the Google Map Tiles API — you'll need a referer-restricted API key with the Map Tiles API enabled.

markerConfig.title: Marker Config
markerConfig.intro: The map.markers.location object lets you replace the default NA map pin with any image or inline SVG.

row.html: HTML rendered inside the marker element. Typically an <img> tag or inline SVG.
row.height: Icon height in pixels. The anchor point is set to the bottom-center of the icon.

exampleWordpress.title: Example: WordPress
exampleWordpress.intro: The easiest way to add Crumb Widget to a WordPress site is the official Crumb plugin (GitHub). It wraps this widget in a shortcode with a settings screen — no code required.

install.1: Search for Crumb in the WordPress plugin directory, or upload the plugin ZIP manually.
install.3: Go to Settings → Crumb and enter your BMLT server URL.
install.4: Add the shortcode to any page or post.

exampleWordpress.serverNote: The BMLT server URL set in the plugin settings is used by default. You can override it — or filter by service body — directly in the shortcode:
exampleWordpress.advancedIntro: All global config options (language, geolocation, columns, map tiles, etc.) can be set from your theme's functions.php via the bmltclient_config filter:

exampleDrupal.title: Example: Drupal
exampleDrupal.intro: Add Crumb Widget to a Drupal 10.3+ or 11 site with the Crumb module. It wraps this widget in a placeable block, a [crumb] shortcode filter for body fields, and a render service for programmatic embedding. Releases are published at github.com/bmlt-enabled/crumb-drupal/releases.

install.1: Download the latest crumb.zip from the releases page.
install.3: Enable: drush en crumb, or via Extend in the admin UI.
install.4: Visit Configuration → Web services → Crumb and enter your BMLT server URL.

exampleDrupal.embedBlockBody: The simplest path: place the Crumb meeting finder block (category: BMLT) in any region via Structure → Block layout. Per-block settings allow overriding server, service body, view, and geolocation.
exampleDrupal.embedShortcode: Embed via shortcode in body fields
exampleDrupal.embedShortcodeBody: Enable the Crumb meeting finder shortcode filter on a text format (Configuration → Content authoring → Text formats and editors), then use:
exampleDrupal.serverNote: The BMLT server URL set in the module settings is used by default. You can override it — or filter by service body — directly in the shortcode:
exampleDrupal.advancedIntro: All global config options (language, geolocation, columns, map tiles, etc.) can be set programmatically via hook_crumb_config_alter() in any custom module:

item.listView: List view — meeting table sorted by day and time, with venue type badges
item.mapView: Map view — interactive Leaflet map for in-person meetings (including those with an online component); click a marker to see meetings at that location; pan or zoom to reveal a Search this area button when Near Me is active
item.detailView: Detail view — full meeting info: schedule, address with Google Maps directions, virtual meeting join button, formats, and notes
item.textSearch: Text search — real-time filter across meeting name, location, and notes
item.weekdayFilter: Weekday filter — toggle individual days of the week
item.venueFilter: Venue type filter — in-person or virtual (meetings with both an in-person location and an online link appear under both)
item.timeFilter: Time of day filter — morning, afternoon, evening, or night
item.formatFilter: Format filter — multi-select dropdown to filter by meeting format (e.g. Open, Closed, Speaker); shows only formats present in the loaded meeting data
item.localeTime: Locale-aware time display — meeting times are shown in 12-hour (AM/PM) or 24-hour format based on the user's browser locale automatically
item.recursive: Recursive service body support — child service bodies are always included
item.multiBody: Multiple service bodies — comma-separate IDs to combine regions
item.print: Printer-friendly list view — when the user prints the page, the widget automatically swaps to a clean black-and-white schedule grouped by weekday, repeats table headers across pages, and prints the full URL for online meetings

routing.title: Routing
routing.intro: By default, the widget uses hash-based routing for meeting detail URLs (e.g. example.com/#/monday-night-meeting-42). This works out of the box on any hosting with no server configuration.
routing.prettyUrlsIntro: For clean URLs without the # fragment, add a data-path attribute with your base path:
routing.prettyUrlsBody: This produces URLs like example.com/meetings/monday-night-meeting-42. Your server must be configured to serve the page for all sub-paths under the base path so that direct visits and page refreshes work:

row.drupal: Set Base Path for Pretty URLs in Configuration → Web services → Crumb; the module registers the necessary route automatically.

routing.iframesP1: When embedding in iframe-based hosts like Google Sites, all in-widget navigation (clicking meetings, back button, sibling links) works normally. However, deep-linking to a specific meeting via URL is not supported because the widget runs inside a proxied iframe and cannot control the parent page's URL.
routing.iframesP2: No special configuration is needed — the default hash-based routing works inside iframes. The data-path attribute is not needed for iframe embeds.

siteBuilders.title: Website Builders
siteBuilders.intro: Crumb Widget can also be embedded on common website builders, but the setup varies by platform.

findServiceBody.title: Finding your Service Body ID
findServiceBody.intro: Every embed below filters meetings to a specific service body (area, region, or zone) by passing its numeric ID in data-service-body. You can look this up from your BMLT server's semantic workshop, which is available at <your-server>/main_server/semantic on every BMLT server.

step.1: Open the semantic workshop in your browser. For example, https://bmlt.sezf.org/main_server/semantic — replace the domain with your own BMLT server.
step.2: Under Operation, choose the action that retrieves service bodies (labels vary slightly by version, but it is typically Get Service Bodies or Retrieve information about Service Bodies).
step.3: Leave the response format as JSON and click Execute JSON Request.
step.4: In the JSON response, find your area, region, or zone by name. The id field next to it is the number you pass to data-service-body.

findServiceBody.outro: In this example you would pass data-service-body="42" for just Greater Seattle, or data-service-body="42,57" to combine both areas. You can also skip the semantic workshop and hit the JSON endpoint directly at <your-server>/main_server/client_interface/json/?switcher=GetServiceBodies.
findServiceBody.callout1: Don't know your server URL or service body ID? Browse tally.bmlt.app — it lists known BMLT servers and the service bodies on each. Find your area, region, or zone to get both the data-server URL and the data-service-body ID in one place.
findServiceBody.callout2: Tip: Child service bodies are always included automatically. If you pass a region's ID, meetings from every area under it are included — you do not need to list each child area.

googleSites.title: Google Sites
googleSites.intro: Google Sites supports embedding HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code using Insert → Embed → Embed code. You can also use a full-page embed if you want the meeting finder to live on its own page.

step.1: Open your site in Google Sites.
step.4: Paste your widget markup and script.

googleSites.note: Note: In Google Sites, JavaScript must be included inside <script> tags when using the embed code option. Because Google Sites runs the widget inside a proxied iframe, direct links to specific meetings (deep-linking) are not supported — but all in-widget navigation works normally.
googleSites.demoLink: See a live demo on Google Sites →

squarespace.title: Squarespace
squarespace.intro: Squarespace embeds use a Code Block, which accepts raw HTML and JavaScript. Per Squarespace's documentation, Code Blocks with JavaScript enabled are available on the Core, Plus, Advanced, Business, Commerce Basic, and Commerce Advanced plans. The free Personal plan does not support JavaScript in Code Blocks.
squarespace.before: Before you start, you need two values:

value.body: data-service-body — the numeric ID for your area, region, or zone (see Finding your Service Body ID)

step.1: Log in to Squarespace and open the page where you want the meeting finder.
step.2: Click Edit, then hover where you want the widget to go and click the + (Add Block) button.
step.3: Search for and insert a Code block.
step.4: In the Code block editor, make sure the Mode dropdown is set to HTML and that Display Source is off (otherwise Squarespace will show the raw tags instead of running the code).
step.5: Paste the snippet below, replacing data-server and data-service-body with your values.
step.6: Click outside the block to close it, then click Save. Publish the page if it is not already live.

squarespace.note: Note: Squarespace renders Code blocks inside the normal page flow, so the widget will take up as much vertical space as it needs. If the preview looks empty, re-open the block and confirm that Display Source is off and the Mode is set to HTML. If you are on a plan that does not allow JavaScript in Code blocks, you will need to upgrade before the widget will load.

wix.title: Wix
wix.intro: Wix splits the embed across two places: the widget's HTML container lives inside your page as an Embed HTML element (Wix calls this "Custom Embeds → Embed HTML" or "Embed a Widget"), and the loader <script> goes in Settings → Custom Code so it only loads once per visit. Per Wix's documentation, Custom Code requires your site to be published on a paid plan with a connected domain.
wix.before: Before you start, you need two values:

value.body: data-service-body — the numeric ID for your area, region, or zone (see Finding your Service Body ID)

wix.step1Heading: Step 1 — Add the loader script (once per site):

step1.1: In your Wix dashboard, go to Settings → Advanced → Custom Code.
step1.3: Paste the loader snippet below into the code box.
step1.4: Under Add Code to Pages, choose either All pages or just the page that will show the widget (loading it only where needed is faster).
step1.6: Name it something like Crumb Widget loader and click Apply.

wix.step2Heading: Step 2 — Add the widget container on the page:

step2.1: Open the page in the Wix Editor.
step2.2: Click + Add Elements → Embed Code → Embed HTML (in Wix Studio this is Embed & Social → HTML iframe/Embed).
step2.3: Drop the element where you want the widget to appear and click Enter Code.
step2.4: Paste the container snippet below, replacing data-server and data-service-body with your values.
step2.5: Click Update. Resize the element to roughly the height you want — Wix's Embed HTML has a fixed size, so give it enough room (e.g. 800px tall) for the meeting list and map.

wix.note: Heads up: Wix's Embed HTML element renders inside an iframe, which means the widget cannot resize the surrounding page and deep-links to individual meetings will not update the browser URL. All in-widget navigation (list, map, meeting detail, filters) still works normally. If you do not see the Custom Code option in Settings, your plan likely does not include it — check that your site is published on a paid plan with a connected domain.

browserSupport.title: Browser Support
browserSupport.intro: All modern browsers. Internet Explorer is not supported.

nothing.title: Nothing is showing up — the widget area is blank
nothing.li1: Wrong server URL. Open data-server in a browser. It should load a BMLT admin page (or at least respond). Double-check the trailing path — most servers expect /main_server/ at the end.
nothing.li2: Missing container. The widget mounts into an element with id="crumb-widget". If the ID is misspelled or the <div> is missing, nothing renders.
nothing.li3: CORS errors. Open your browser's developer console (F12). If you see a CORS error, your BMLT server is not allowing requests from your site's domain — contact your server admin.
nothing.li4: Container has no height. If you set data-view="map" or data-view="both", the parent container needs a height. Add style="height:600px" to the <div>.

map.title: The map is blank or won't load
map.li1: No meetings have coordinates. The map only shows meetings with valid latitude/longitude on your BMLT server. Check the meetings in your BMLT admin.
map.li2: Tile provider blocked. If your site has a strict Content-Security-Policy, make sure tile URLs (default: tile.openstreetmap.org) are allowed in img-src and connect-src.
map.li3: Geolocation denied. "Near me" search requires the user to grant location permission in their browser. If denied, the search falls back to text/postal-code search.

colors.title: How do I change the colors to match my site?
colors.body: Override the widget's CSS custom properties — see CSS Variables. For dark mode, see Dark Mode. You can also use one of the built-in helper themes — see CSS Helper Classes.

serviceBody.title: My service body filter shows no meetings — why?
serviceBody.li1: Wrong service body ID. The ID must be the numeric ID from your BMLT server, not the name. See Finding your Service Body ID.
serviceBody.li2: No published meetings. The service body may exist but have no active/published meetings on the server.
serviceBody.li3: Recursion is always on. Child service bodies are automatically included — this cannot be disabled. If you want only one specific body, that body must be a leaf (no children) on the server.

iframe.title: Can I embed this inside an iframe (Google Sites, etc.)?
iframe.body: Yes. Set data-router="hash" so individual meeting links use #-style URLs, which work inside iframes where the parent page controls the address bar. See Routing and the Google Sites example.

help.title: I'm still stuck — where can I get help?
help.li1: Bug or feature request: open an issue on GitHub.

npmPackage.title: npm Package
npmPackage.intro: In addition to the CDN script-tag embed, Crumb Widget is available as an npm package for use in JavaScript/TypeScript projects (React, Svelte, Vue, plain Vite, etc.).
npmPackage.peerDep: leaflet is a peer dependency — install it alongside crumb-widget so consumers can dedupe and pin their preferred version.
npmPackage.usageIntro: Import mountCrumbWidget and call it with a DOM element and your configuration. The widget mounts itself and injects its own CSS — no separate stylesheet import needed.
npmPackage.svelteIntro: In a Svelte component, use onMount to ensure the element exists before mounting:
npmPackage.optionsIntro: All options from Global Config are available, plus two additional required fields:

row.serviceBodyIds: Service body IDs to filter by. Omit or pass [] to show all.
row.view: Initial view. Defaults to 'list'. 'both' shows map above list with no toggle.
row.geolocation: Enable the Near Me button. Defaults to false.
row.geolocationRadius: Maximum search radius (caps the distance dropdown). Defaults to 75.
row.distanceOptions: Distance values shown in the Near Me dropdown. Defaults to [5,10,15,25,50,100].
row.distanceUnit: Unit label for distances. Defaults to 'mi'.
row.language: BCP-47 language tag (e.g. 'es'). Defaults to browser locale.
row.columns: Table columns to display. See Global Config.
row.map: Tile provider and marker config. See Tile Provider.

npmPackage.note: Note: The npm package bundles Svelte and all styles into a single JS file. leaflet is a peer dependency (install it alongside crumb-widget) so you can dedupe with any other Leaflet-using code in your app. Each call to mountCrumbWidget is independent, but multiple widgets on the same page are not currently supported.

philosophy.title: Philosophy
philosophy.body: This project aims to cover the needs of most NA service bodies well — not every possible use case. It is intentionally kept simple and focused. New features are weighed against the cost to the codebase; niche requests that bloat the project for everyone are generally out of scope. If you need deep customization, this may not be the right tool.
